title: PasteBin mobile filename overflow fixes + home/paste UI polish

requirements.summary: Prevent long filenames from overflowing on mobile across selected-files list, upload progress card, and paste file list using responsive flex/min-width and truncation/wrapping rules.
acceptanceCriteria: On ~360px-wide screens, long filenames remain fully contained within the card/list row without horizontal scrolling.
CreatePastePage selected-files rows remain readable on mobile (filename truncates or wraps) and action buttons (remove) remain reachable without pushing content outside the container.
UploadProgressStatus filename line does not expand the card beyond the viewport; the filename truncates cleanly on small screens.
PasteViewPage file rows remain within the viewport on mobile; if necessary, the Download button stacks below the filename on small screens while remaining aligned inline on larger screens.

file_operations.description: Update the selected-files row layout to be mobile-safe: ensure flex children use min-w-0, apply truncation or wrap-safe word breaking to filenames, and allow responsive stacking (e.g., row becomes column on small screens) so the remove button remains reachable without pushing content outside the container. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Harden the upload progress status card against filename overflow: add min-w-0 to the filename container, keep the filename line truncating within available space, and ensure the percentage label cannot force horizontal overflow on narrow screens. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Make paste file rows responsive: ensure filename container uses min-w-0 with truncation/word-break, and on small screens switch to a stacked layout where the Download button can move below the filename while remaining inline-aligned on larger screens. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Polish the home/create page UI (route “/”) with improved hierarchy, spacing, and typography while preserving all existing functionality and wording intent across light/dark mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Home/create page presents a clearer hierarchy (distinct hero/intro vs form area) with consistent spacing and alignment.
No functionality changes: users can still enter a message, upload files, select expiration, and create a paste as before.
Design remains consistent and polished in both light and dark themes (no low-contrast text, no clashing surfaces).

file_operations.description: Refine CreatePastePage layout and visual hierarchy: clearly separate hero/intro content from the create form area (spacing, section headings, consistent alignment), improve typography scale and rhythm, and adjust surfaces/borders to look more professional while keeping all flows and wording intent intact (including unauthenticated and success states). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Polish shared layout spacing and header/footer presentation to better support the updated home page hierarchy (e.g., container padding, header visual weight) while keeping navigation behavior unchanged and ensuring light/dark cohesion. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Apply a consistent, modern visual theme across the home/create page and paste view page with uniform card/border/spacing/button styling, without changing behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Cards, headings, descriptions, and action buttons follow a consistent styling approach (spacing, border treatment, and emphasis states).
No new feature behavior is introduced; changes are purely presentational.
The updated styling does not reduce usability on mobile (tap targets remain usable and content remains legible).

file_operations.description: Introduce small, reusable theme utilities (e.g., a shared card/surface class and consistent section spacing helpers) using existing Tailwind layers so CreatePastePage and PasteViewPage can share a coherent border/elevation/typography treatment in both light and dark mode. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Adopt the shared theme utilities for cards/sections/buttons on the home/create page to ensure consistent surfaces, borders, and emphasis states while keeping all behavior unchanged. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Adopt the shared theme utilities for the paste view page (card, section headings, file rows, action buttons) to match the home/create page styling, ensuring mobile tap targets and readability remain strong. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
